### Title

ROC Curve for a 2% CER Threshold

### Purpose of the Figure

Shows the various entropy thresholds along with their TPRs and FPRs to determine what entropy threshold is most representative of determining if a page has a CER < 2%.

### Generated By

[roc_thresholds.py](../../scripts/roc_thresholds.py)

### Input Files Used

[results_k_10.csv](../../results/csv/results_k_10.csv)

### Output Filenames

PNG: [figure_06_roc_entropy.png](../../results/figures/figure_06_roc_entropy.png)

SVG: [figure_06_roc_entropy.svg](../../results/figures/figure_06_roc_entropy.svg)

### X-axis

False Positive Rate

### Y-axis

True Positive Rate

### Units

False Positive Rate: scalar

True Positive Rate: scalar

### Other Information

Filtering rules: samples with a CER > 2% were filtered from the dataset.

Normalization choices: whitespaces were normalized, punctuation was removed entirely, and all characters were converted to lowercase between ground-truth and ocr texts.

Sample size: the plot illustrates the ROC for 20 randomized-page excerpts which is the same as the validation set size after splitting the original 100 excerpts into an 80/20 training-test split.

### Figure Importance

This figure helps determine the best entropy threshold and how uncertain GPT-4o has to be to produce an incorrect OCR transcript for an image.
